keyMyths.title: Genesis (Abrahamic Religions)
keyMyths.description: God creates the universe, Earth, and all living things in six days, resting on the seventh. Humanity is created last and given dominion over creation, but falls from grace after disobeying God's command.

keyMyths.title: Enuma Elish (Mesopotamian Mythology)
keyMyths.description: From the primordial waters of Apsu and Tiamat, the gods are born. Conflict arises, culminating in Marduk defeating Tiamat and using her body to create the sky and the earth, establishing order from chaos.

keyMyths.title: Popol Vuh (Mayan Mythology)
keyMyths.description: The gods attempt multiple times to create humans from different materials (mud, wood) before finally succeeding with maize. These maize people become the ancestors of the Mayan people and are tasked with worshipping the gods.

keyMyths.title: Theogony (Greek Mythology)
keyMyths.description: From Chaos emerges Gaia (Earth), Tartarus (the Underworld), and Eros (Desire). Gaia gives birth to Uranus (Sky), and their offspring, the Titans, battle the Olympian gods for control of the cosmos, with Zeus ultimately emerging victorious.

keyMyths.title: Rigveda (Hindu Mythology)
keyMyths.description: The Nasadiya Sukta questions the very origin of the universe, positing that before creation, there was neither existence nor non-existence. The universe arises from a primal sacrifice (Purusha Sukta), where a cosmic being is dismembered to form the world and its social order.
keyMyths.source: Rigveda, Nasadiya Sukta (RV 10.129) and Purusha Sukta (RV 10.90)

sources.name: Mircea Eliade, *Cosmos and History: The Myth of the Eternal Return*
sources.description: Explores the concept of cyclical time and the reenactment of creation myths in rituals.
sources.title: Mircea Eliade, *Cosmos and History: The Myth of the Eternal Return*

sources.name: Joseph Campbell, *The Hero with a Thousand Faces*
sources.description: Discusses the monomyth and the shared structural elements across various creation narratives.
sources.title: Joseph Campbell, *The Hero with a Thousand Faces*

sources.name: David Leeming, *Creation Myths of the World*
sources.description: A comprehensive collection of creation myths from diverse cultures around the globe.
sources.title: David Leeming, *Creation Myths of the World*

sources.name: G.S. Kirk, *Myth: Its Meaning and Functions in Ancient and Other Cultures*
sources.description: Analyzes the functions and interpretations of myths, including creation narratives, in various societies.
sources.title: G.S. Kirk, *Myth: Its Meaning and Functions in Ancient and Other Cultures*

sources.name: Wendy Doniger O'Flaherty, *Hindu Myths*
sources.description: A comprehensive collection of Hindu myths, including several different creation stories.
sources.title: Wendy Doniger O'Flaherty, *Hindu Myths*
